zh.lifeSummary: 當Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury於1619年7月出生在美國密西根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦時，他的父親William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC28歲，母親Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury31歲。他於1639年4月2日與Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1643年登記服兵役。他於1640年被列為member of parliament for hertford。他去世於1660年12月在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里 馮 克洛斯 本 馮 卡農斯 本 馮 凱德拉爾 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury于1619年7月出生在美国密歇根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦时，他的父亲William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC28岁，母亲Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury31岁。他于1639年4月2日与Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1643年登记服兵役。他于1640年被列为member of parliament for hertford。他去世于1660年12月在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年41岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里 馮 克洛斯 本 馮 卡農斯 本 馮 凱德拉爾 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Cecil,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Old Welsh personal name Seisyllt, sometimes wrongly said to derive from the Latin name Caecilius (see Sisley ). The most frequent modern spelling is a Renaissance attempt to connect the surname with Caecilius in the English pronunciation of its time. The Welsh name is more probably, but not definitely, from the Latin name Sextilius, a derivative of sextus ‘sixth’.

History: The great and powerful English Cecil family first came to prominence with David Cecil, a Monmouthshire gentleman who espoused the cause of Henry Tudor and came to court in London after the latter became king in 1485. His grandson William Cecil, Lord Burghley (1520–98), was Elizabeth I's chief adviser for 40 years, and his descendants have remained politically powerful and culturally influential in Britain ever since. They were originally minor Welsh gentry; their name is found in a variety of forms, including Sitsylt, Ceyssel, and Sisseld.

variants.name: Cissell
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of Welsh   Cecil  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

variants.name: Cissel
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Cecil  .

nameMeanings.name: Second
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Sicard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Secor and Sicord .
History: The Secords are descendants of Ambroise Sicard, a Huguenot refugee from France (see   Sicard  ).

variants.name: Sicard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Albertine, Aude, Benoit, Camille, Elzear, Francois, Jacques, Jean-Paul, Marcel, Monique, Numa.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sighard, composed of the elements sig ‘victory’ and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Seacord , Secor , Secord , Secore , Secoy , and Sicord .

History: Jean Sicard from Mornac-sur-Seudre in Charente-Maritime, France, married Catherine Lauzon in Montreal, QC, in 1681. Jean-Baptiste Sicard, sieur de Carufel (see Carufel ), from Castres in Tarn, France, married Geneviève Raté in Saint-Pierre, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1694. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Sicord and Seacord) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor was Ambroise Sicard from France, who arrived with his wife and children in NY in the 1680s via England. His descendants (also) bear the surnames Seacord, Secor, Secord, and Secoy.

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Salisbury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name primarily from Salesbury in Blackburn (Lancashire) but also occasionally from Salisbury (Wiltshire). The Lancashire placename derives from Old English salh ‘willow, sallow’ + burg ‘fortress’, while the Wiltshire placename arises from a shortened form of the Celtic placename Sorviodunum (from an unknown initial element + Celtic dūno- ‘fort’). In the Old English period the second element was dropped and Sorvio- (of unexplained etymology) became Searo- in Old English as the result of folk etymological association the Old English word searu ‘trick’; to this an explanatory burh ‘fortress, manor, town’ was added. The city is recorded in the Domesday Book as Sarisberie; the change of -r- to -l- is the result of later dissimilation.

variants.name: Saulsbury
meanings: English: variant of   Salisbury  .

variants.name: Saulsberry
meanings: Altered form of English   Salisbury  .

variants.name: Salsbery
meanings: English: variant of   Salisbury  .

variants.name: Salsbury
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of   Salisbury  .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of Salomon or some other Jewish name beginning with Sa-.

variants.name: Salsberry
meanings: English: variant of   Salisbury  .

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury was born in July 1619, in London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, United States, his father, William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC, was 28 and his mother, Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, was 31. He married Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne on 2 April 1639, in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. He registered for military service in 1643. In 1640, his occupation is listed as member of parliament for hertford. He died in December 1660, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 41, and was buried in Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury est né en juillet 1619, à London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, États-Unis, son père, William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne le 2 avril 1639, à Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1643. En 1640, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament for hertford. Il est décédé en décembre 1660, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury im Juli 1619 in London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC 28 und seine Mutter Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury 31. Er hat am 2. April 1639 in Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er hat sich 1643 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1640 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament for hertford aufgeführt. Er starb im Dezember 1660 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England und wurde in Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.Saint Martin in the Fields, Westminster,: St Martin in the Fields, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito
it.Salisbury Cathedral, Salisbury, Wiltshir: Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito
it.Westminster Abbey, London, Middlesex, En: London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America
it.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury è nato il nel luglio 1619 nel London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne il 2 aprile 1639, nel Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 7 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1643. Nel 1640, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament for hertford. Morì nel dicembre 1660, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolto a Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisburyが1619年7月にアメリカ合衆国ミシガン州モンローロンドン トウンシュイップロンドンで生まれた時，父William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PCは28歳，母Catherine Howard Countess of Salisburyは31歳であった。1639年4月2日にイギリスイギリスロンドンウエストミンスターでLady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1643年に兵役登録をした。1640年，職業はmember of parliament for hertfordと記録されている。彼は1660年12月にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリー ス クロゼ オフ ス カノンス オフ ス カスエドラル チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Martin in the Fields, Westminster,: 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스트 마틴 인 테 필드즈

ko.Westminster Abbey, London, Middlesex, En: 미국 미시간 주 먼로 런던 타운시프 런던
ko.lifeSummary: Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury(이)가 1619년 7월 미국 미시간 주 먼로 런던 타운시프 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1639년 4월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터에서 Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1643년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1640년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament for hertford이었다. 그는 1660년 12월 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이 테 클로스 프 테 캔스 프 테 캐테드럴 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury nasceu em julho de 1619, em London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne em 2 de abril de 1639, em Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1643. Em 1640, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament for hertford. Ele faleceu em dezembro de 1660, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Martin in the Fields, Westminster,: ст мартинь ин т фиелдс, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Salisbury Cathedral, Salisbury, Wiltshir: салисбури т клос оф т канонс оф т кэтедраль чурч, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Westminster Abbey, London, Middlesex, En: лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury родился июль 1619 г. в местности лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne 2 апреля 1639 г. в местности вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. В 1643 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1640 годуработал по профессии member of parliament for hertford. Он умер декабрь 1660 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности салисбури т клос оф т канонс оф т кэтедраль чурч, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Reverend Lord Charles Cecil, Second Earl of Salisbury nació en julio de 1619, en London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos, su papá, William Cecil 2nd Earl of Salisbury KG PC, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Catherine Howard Countess of Salisbury, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Lady Diana Maxwell Viscountess of Cranbourne el 2 de abril de 1639, en Westminster, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1643. En 1640, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament for hertford. Murió en diciembre de 1660, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Salisbury the Close of the Canons of the Cathedral Church, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
